mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, it signals severe labor market distress and weak consumer spending power, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent, or Rank IC falls below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer confidence and economic recovery, which jointly drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal in RSAFS and HOUST yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth shows no predictive power for 5-day returns during recovery phases, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product confirms sustained economic expansion and robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate profit growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate loses predictive significance over the 60-day horizon, with Rank IC dropping below 0.02 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, it indicates accelerating inflation that forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 215.0 fail to correlate with negative 20-day forward returns, or positive returns exceed 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumer spending, jointly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect renewed confidence in the residential construction sector and broader economic activity, which supports financial sector earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to predict 60-day returns in the recovery regime, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 or positive return frequency dropping below 50 percent.
